label: The Escape Artist
gift: Honoring the wisdom of fear — finding the exit, the trapdoor, the well-timed retreat.

cards.name: Notice what your fear is protecting — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: the wisdom of fear, sensing what is here.
steps: Notice what is actually here — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: what your fear is protecting.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I not letting myself notice here?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does this situation need someone to see?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing what is actually there before reacting — your the wisdom of fear at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Reacting before you have actually looked
Sliding into the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.
failureModes: Blind spots you swear aren't there
cards.remediation: Name one thing you hadn't let yourself see.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.

cards.name: Spot Your Dodge
cards.essence: Notice the step you're avoiding because doing it makes the need real.
steps: Scan for the task you keep sliding past.
Feel the avoidance in your body.
Name the dodge out loud and take the smallest next step.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I not letting myself see because seeing it commits me?
cards.campaignQuestion: What key step am I quietly dodging?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing the avoided move before it silently sinks the effort.
forbiddenMoves: Calling the dodge 'being busy'
Guilt-spiraling instead of acting
Vanishing into research
cards.remediation: Open the avoided thing for 5 minutes only.

tell.working: You name the dodge and touch it.
tell.performed: You make a new to-do list to avoid the old one.

contraindications: When you genuinely need rest, not another push
cards.example: You keep 'forgetting' the key task; you open it for five minutes.

cards.name: Feel the fear without obeying it — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: the wisdom of fear, sensing what is here.
steps: Receive it without flinching — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: the fear without obeying it.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I actually feeling, underneath?
cards.campaignQuestion: What is the other person or group really reaching for?
cards.optimizesFor: Receiving the charge instead of bracing against it — your the wisdom of fear at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Numbing or performing the feeling instead of feeling it
Sliding into the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.
cards.remediation: Let it land for one breath before you respond.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.

cards.name: Receive Without Bracing
cards.essence: Let help land without bracing for the catch.
steps: Notice the brace in your body when someone offers.
Breathe; let the offer in one inch further than feels safe.
Say a clean 'thank you' without deflecting.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I protecting by refusing help — and is it still worth the cost?
cards.campaignQuestion: Whose offered help am I deflecting out of habit?
cards.optimizesFor: Actually receiving the support people offer.
forbiddenMoves: Deflecting the offer
Over-explaining why you don't really need it
Attaching strings to received help
failureModes: Turning help into a debt you must repay
cards.remediation: If you deflect, circle back: 'Actually — yes, that would help.'

tell.performed: You accept but immediately plan to pay it back double.

contraindications: When the 'help' has real strings you're right to refuse
cards.example: Someone offers to pitch in; instead of 'oh you don't have to,' say 'thank you — that genuinely helps.'

cards.name: Wise Retreat or Just Scared?
cards.essence: Sort a genuine reason not to act from plain exposure-fear.
steps: Feel the flinch and locate it in the body.
Ask the Escape Artist's question: what is this fear protecting?
Sort: a real reason (name it) vs. just being seen needing help (proceed anyway).
cards.primaryQuestion: Is this 'no' wisdom, or is it just fear wearing wisdom's coat?
cards.campaignQuestion: Where am I calling avoidance 'discernment'?
cards.optimizesFor: Not abandoning a good move because being seen needing help feels exposing.
forbiddenMoves: Dressing avoidance as discernment
Going quiet to dodge the feeling
Carrying others' guilt about your need
cards.remediation: Set a 10-minute timer; take one real step before it rings.

tell.working: You name the fear and move.
tell.performed: You generate three more reasons to wait.

contraindications: When the retreat really is the wise move — then rest without guilt
cards.example: You feel dread about posting the ask; you sort it: not danger, just exposure — you post.

cards.name: Sort the false exit from the real one — Sense
cards.essence: For others: the wisdom of fear, sensing what is here.
steps: Transform the charge — sensing what is here.
Bring it to others: the false exit from the real one.
cards.primaryQuestion: What charge am I carrying that needs to move?
cards.campaignQuestion: What needs to be metabolized before we move together?
cards.optimizesFor: Metabolizing the charge so it stops leaking — your the wisdom of fear at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Venting the charge onto someone else
Sliding into the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.
cards.remediation: Name what you are feeling out loud before you act.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.

cards.name: Build your tolerance for staying — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: the wisdom of fear, sensing what is here.
steps: Build the capacity — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: your tolerance for staying.
cards.primaryQuestion: Who do I need to become for this?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity does this work need us to build?
cards.optimizesFor: Building the capacity the work keeps asking for — your the wisdom of fear at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Forcing growth or skipping the rep
Sliding into the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.
cards.remediation: Pick one small rep and do it once.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.

cards.name: Open the exit you actually need — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: the wisdom of fear, sensing what is here.
steps: Take the concrete act — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: the exit you actually need.
cards.primaryQuestion: What is the one act I keep avoiding?
cards.campaignQuestion: What concrete act would actually help right now?
cards.optimizesFor: Taking the one concrete act that moves things — your the wisdom of fear at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Hinting or prepping forever instead of acting
Sliding into the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.
failureModes: The move that never quite happens
cards.remediation: Do the smallest real version today.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Perpetual Vanisher — leaving before it’s time.

cards.name: Stay for the Ask
cards.essence: Don’t vanish in the sixty seconds before you make the ask.
steps: Feel the urge to flee right before you act.
Set a 10-minute timer; name the fear out loud.
Make one ask before it rings.
cards.primaryQuestion: Is my urge to bolt protecting me from real danger, or just from being seen needing help?
cards.campaignQuestion: Which ask am I one click away from making but keep escaping?
cards.optimizesFor: Making the ask — the move that could actually increase life — instead of fleeing it.
forbiddenMoves: Calling avoidance 'timing'
Suddenly reorganizing your whole plan to avoid acting
Pressuring yourself into freeze
cards.remediation: Shrink it: make the ask to one safe person first.

tell.working: You name the fear and act anyway.
tell.performed: You produce three more reasons to wait.

contraindications: When pausing is genuinely wiser — but be honest
cards.example: Timer set: make one ask before 10 minutes pass.

cards.name: Pre-Commit the Rest
cards.essence: Decide in advance when you'll rest, so you don't bolt mid-push.
steps: Name the point where you'll pause (after the work goes out, not before).
Schedule the rest like an appointment.
When the urge to vanish hits early, feel it, point to the scheduled rest, and keep going.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I about to call quitting 'self-care' to escape the exposure?
cards.campaignQuestion: What sustainable rhythm lets me keep showing up without burning out or bolting?
cards.optimizesFor: Staying in the work by resourcing rest, so the effort can increase life rather than drain you.
forbiddenMoves: Disguising flight as self-care
Pushing with no rest until you crash
Ghosting without a return plan
cards.remediation: If you bolted, name the real fear and set the next small step.

tell.working: You rest on schedule and return.
tell.performed: You 'rest' the moment it gets exposing.

contraindications: When you truly need to stop — then stop, cleanly
cards.example: Plan: do the next batch of work, then take the weekend off, then resume Monday.

steps: Take the concrete act — building it into structure.

cards.name: Name the Cost of Staying
cards.essence: Name what staying in the work actually costs you, so the cost is chosen, not leaked.
steps: Feel where the work drains you.
Name the real cost out loud (time, pride, exposure).
Decide if it's worth it — and if yes, stay on purpose.
cards.primaryQuestion: What is staying in this costing me that I haven't named?
cards.campaignQuestion: What do I need to acknowledge about the cost so I can keep showing up honestly?
cards.optimizesFor: Chosen, sustainable presence instead of silent depletion.
forbiddenMoves: Pretending it costs nothing
Becoming a martyr
Turning your cost into others' obligation
cards.remediation: Name the cost to one trusted person; let it be witnessed.

tell.working: You stay clear-eyed about the price and stay anyway.
tell.performed: You keep score of what staying costs.

contraindications: When the cost is too high — then it's okay to step back
cards.example: Admit the work bruises your pride; name it, and choose to keep going.
